_publications._publicationTitle: Development of 3D culture models of plexiform neurofibroma and initial application for phenotypic characterization and drug screening

_contextSnippet: Plates centrifuged at 2000 × g, 4 min, 4°C...to reduce meniscus and create even spread of rBM. If layer too thin cells migrate to plastic and assume 2D monolayer morphology.
details: Key protocol tips: (1) Centrifuge rBM-coated plates at 2,000×g for 4 min at 4°C to level the matrix and eliminate meniscus; (2) Matrix layer must be ≥1 mm thick — if too thin, cells migrate to plastic and revert to 2D monolayer morphology; (3) Incubate plates on the lowest incubator shelf (highest humidity) before adding media; (4) 3D cultures require higher cell plating density than 2D (e.g., 8,000 cells/well for MEK inhibitor assays); media changed on day 4, endpoint at day 6
